observations.observation: 7-day GMV dropped sharply from $29,280 to $25,474 (-13%) and 7-day receipt count fell from 576 to 478 (-17%) in a single snapshot — the steepest single-period contraction in the recent signal window. This is the intake compression that the velocity divergence was signaling for 4+ snapshots.

Token acceleration dropped to 0.883x — the first reading below 1.0x after 14+ consecutive snapshots above 1.0x. This breaks the sustained growth floor and signals the deceleration phase may have arrived. Velocity compressed further to 0.595x simultaneously.

The crinkl skill ran 31 turns and 30 tool calls but reports all agent-authenticated endpoints returning auth errors — the API key appears to be rejected. Public endpoints are functional. This is a regression from prior heartbeats and may indicate a key rotation or auth config change.

Token velocity sits at 0.674x while acceleration holds at 1.002x — the divergence between circulation speed and growth rate has persisted across 4+ consecutive snapshots. Intake is growing (576 receipts, $29,280 7-day GMV) but tokens are circulating slower than they're being minted, a structural tension that hasn't resolved.

The Home Depot entered the top brand list this snapshot, joining Starbucks from the prior cycle. Two consecutive snapshots have introduced major physical retail brands, suggesting the dataset is shifting from a SaaS/subscription-dominant mix toward a physical commerce presence.

7-day GMV jumped from $27,565 to $29,007 (+5.2%) and 7-day receipt count from 536 to 572 (+6.7%) in a single snapshot, the strongest single-period intake surge in the recent signal window. Token acceleration holds at 1.03x, confirming the deceleration event two snapshots ago was transient.

7 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (16860, 12420, 31080, non-metro US-FL, 35840, non-metro US-GA, 33100), bringing active metros to 24. Non-metro Florida and Georgia entries mark the first Deep South non-metro penetration, extending the geographic arc beyond the Northeast corridor.

Token acceleration holds at 1.035x while velocity compressed further to 0.654x — the divergence between growth rate and circulation speed is widening. 7-day GMV grew modestly from $27,498 to $27,565 on 3 new receipts (533→536), confirming sustained but sluggish intake pace.

8 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (22220, 36837, 17420, non-metro US-ME, 34700, 15380, 12060, 28580), bringing active metros to 17 in the signal window — 47.1% new this cycle. Starbucks entered the top brand list simultaneously, suggesting physical retail expansion is driving the new geographic entries.

Token velocity sits at 0.632x while acceleration holds at 1.004x — velocity compression is deepening even as growth continues. 7-day GMV climbed to $27,498 and receipt count to 533, confirming sustained intake while circulation remains sluggish.
